other_observers: Mark Peterson; first found by Mymm Ackley about one week earlier
specific_location: about 7 miles south of Hwy 78 on the 3R road
description: This is a straight forward identification.  This was and all gray bird with a ragged gray crest and a red eye. When it flew the bird showed a long tail and rounded wings.  The bird was a slender and was a little smaller than the Ash-throated Flycather that was nearby.Behaviors: Often perched on top of dead twigs mostly in midstory of bushes but on occassion on top of a one-seeded juniper. Had a jerky flight as if having difficulty flying, especially in response to a tape of its song.   Sometimes perched only a few feet off of ground but almost always on dead branches where it was visible for long periods of time.Call: on occassion would respond to the tape of its song with a soft songPlumage: alternate
